missed_changes: New JTO (Jito) token investment on Solana to address MEV — a specific new token bet not anticipated
Escalated privacy from one of several sectors to THE central thesis: 'privacy will be the most important moat,' winner-take-most/chain lock-in framing
New lead investment in Seismic ($10M round) and new partnership with Zodl on privacy-finance
Newly elaborated 'finance first, consumer crypto later' thesis explaining why gaming/social haven't taken off
New explicit defense of token value mechanics — tokens compound as claims on future protocol cashflows (vs. the prediction's stance of not making token/price arguments)
Quantum computing threat to crypto raised as a new topic of interest
Significant non-market content: founder psychology (open/closed mode), habit-building, and political commentary (California wealth tax)
notes: Nearly all predicted sector and token stances were confirmed, with privacy/ZK by far the most heavily reinforced theme. DePIN went unaddressed in the window (Gensyn appears but reframed as AI-funding, not physical infrastructure; Daylight only named in passing). Memecoins-as-category not restated, though the related casino/airdrop-farming concern surfaces via criticism of 'mercenary' airdrop farmers. Risk-regime no-view stance held — he continued to focus on long-term technology and fundamentals rather than market timing. Note he did newly argue tokens compound as cashflow claims, which is more price/value-oriented than the prediction's 'I don't make price calls' framing, though still framed structurally.
